findings.title: queryEmbedding is undefined at runtime if generateEmbeddings returns an empty array
findings.why_it_matters: If generateEmbeddings resolves (no throw) but returns an empty array [], embeddings[0] is undefined at runtime. TypeScript types queryEmbedding as Float32Array without noUncheckedIndexedAccess and does not flag the assignment. Execution then falls through to line 79: vectorStore.search(Array.from(queryEmbedding), 5). Array.from(undefined) throws TypeError: undefined is not iterable — past the try-catch boundary, so the new fallback cannot intercept it. The caller sees a crash, not a graceful degradation. EmbedResult.embeddings is typed as Float32Array[] (unconstrained array, not a tuple), so the worker could legally return [] in a success response without triggering the catch. Pre-existing because the original code const [queryEmbedding] = await generateEmbeddings([query.trim()]) had identical undefined behavior on an empty array response.
evidence: src/lib/noteQA.ts line 55: let queryEmbedding: Float32Array — uninitialized declaration, no default value
src/lib/noteQA.ts line 58: queryEmbedding = embeddings[0] — no array-bounds guard before indexing
src/ai/workers/types.ts line 70: embeddings: Float32Array[] — not a non-empty tuple type
src/lib/noteQA.ts line 79: vectorStore.search(Array.from(queryEmbedding), 5) — called unconditionally after the try-catch exits
coordinator.ts terminate() rejects in-flight requests when tab is hidden — confirms the throw path is covered; the silent-empty-array success path is not

findings.title: tokenize regex is ASCII-only; non-ASCII queries silently return no results in the fallback path
findings.why_it_matters: tokenize('/[a-z0-9]+/g') silently drops every non-ASCII character. For a query such as 'réaction' (French with accent), 'проект' (Russian), or '反応' (Japanese), queryTerms.length === 0 after tokenization. retrieveRelevantNotesByText then immediately returns [] via the early-exit guard. The user receives zero results with no error — they cannot distinguish 'no matching notes' from 'fallback is broken for their language'. The semantic path (embedding-based) handles all of these correctly; only the new fallback is broken for non-ASCII input.
evidence: src/lib/noteQA.ts line 133: value.toLowerCase().match(/[a-z0-9]+/g) ?? [] — character class restricted to ASCII [a-z0-9]
src/lib/noteQA.ts line 105: if (queryTerms.length === 0) return [] — silent empty return with no warning logged
Test coverage (noteQA.test.ts lines 248-261) only exercises ASCII queries ('react hooks', 'hi') — no non-ASCII fallback test exists

findings.title: phraseBoost can push fallback similarity above 1.0, producing out-of-range scores relative to the semantic path
findings.why_it_matters: matchedTerms.length / queryTerms.length is bounded to [0, 1]. Adding phraseBoost (0.25 when the phrase appears verbatim) raises the ceiling to 1.25. The semantic path returns cosine similarity; src/ai/workers/types.ts line 80 explicitly documents score as 'Cosine similarity 0-1'. RetrievedNote.similarity is typed as number without documented bounds, so callers that mix results from both paths may sort or display incorrectly if they assume [0,1] scale. This does not break ranking within the fallback alone but could be confusing if a future caller displays percentage-style similarity or gates on a threshold near 1.0.
evidence: src/lib/noteQA.ts line 124: const similarity = matchedTerms.length / queryTerms.length + phraseBoost — additive with no clamping
src/ai/workers/types.ts line 80: score: number // Cosine similarity 0-1 — documents the semantic path as bounded

residual_risks: If generateEmbeddings can resolve with an empty array without throwing (e.g., embedding worker returns a structurally valid but content-empty EmbedResult), the undefined-queryEmbedding path is not covered by the new catch block and will still surface as a TypeError crash to the caller.

The catch block swallows all errors from generateEmbeddings, including transient failures (network, worker OOM, 5 s timeout) that might succeed on retry. There is no retry logic before the fallback is triggered; users with intermittent worker failures will always get downgraded text search results without knowing a retry was possible.

DB errors inside retrieveRelevantNotesByText (db.notes.toArray() rejection) propagate correctly and are not swallowed, but they surface after the embedding failure has already been logged as a warning — a second error appearing in the console after a warning may confuse diagnostics.
testing_gaps: No test for generateEmbeddings resolving with [] (empty array without throwing) — the new catch only covers rejected promises; the silent-empty-success path is unexercised.
No test for a non-ASCII query in the text fallback (tokenize returns [] silently, function returns [] with no error).
No test verifying db.notes.toArray() rejection during the fallback path propagates as a thrown error rather than being swallowed.
No test verifying that similarity values from the text fallback are in [0, 1] — would catch the phraseBoost overflow without clamping.
